meaning: ISCED 2011 simplified: highest completed degree of education
question: EDULEVEL is compiled from the variable ISCED by truncation. Reducing ISCED to the first digit takes EDULEVEL closer to an ordinal concept of educational achievement.

NO.7: Master’s or equivalent level (including doctoral or equivalent degree)

meaning: Currently, formerly, or never in paid work
question: Are you currently working for pay, did you work for pay in the past, or have you never been in paid work?

content.2: Currently not in paid work, paid work in the past

DK.2: Currently not in paid work, paid work in the past (including unemployed, laid off, looking for work)

US.2: Currently not in paid work, paid work in the past (including unemployed, laid off, looking for work)

question: How many hours, on average, do you usually work for pay in a normal week, including overtime?
Are/ were you an employee, self-employed, or working for your own family's business?

content.3: Self-employed with 1 to 9 employees; SR: Self-empl. with 1 to 11 empl.
content.4: Self-employed with 10 employees or more; SR: Self-empl. with 12 or more

CN.-9: No answer (agricultural work not counted as paid work)

question: Do/ did you supervise other employees?

content.-4: NAP (Code 3 in WORK; US: Code 2,3 in WORK)

CN.-9: No answer (agricultural work not counted as paid work)

question: How many other employees do/ did you supervise?

content.30: 30 employees; NL, SR: More than 25 subordinates

CN.-9: No answer (agricultural work not counted as paid work)

question: Do/did you work for a for-profit organisation or for a non-profit organisation?

CN.-9: No answer (agricultural work not counted as paid work)

FR.1: For-profit organisation (publicly owned firm, private firm, self-employed, family's business)
FR.2: Non-profit organisation (government or local communities, association, mutual company, cooperative)
